description: PRODUCTION FLAGGING C2 - assist response surfaces flags + client UX (combines gap FGAP-008 / C2). (1) views.py result frame (AssistStreamView ~:506) includes the parser flags (A3) alongside prefill/note; (2) ai-assist.js routes a flags payload to per-element annotations on .planner-fs-row rows (_formset.html data-row index) - badge/highlight; (3) a per-flag resolution affordance: accept / replace (catalogued value) / request-catalogue-addition. DISC-30/DISC-31 UX-layer closure (admin sees an actionable signal, not event:error). Depends on A3 (TASK-018, AssistResult.flags) + C1 (TASK-019, if resolution persists to the Flag model). Part of the three-valued flagging model, production layer. Build via the canonical pipeline.
acceptance_criteria: When an assist response carries flags, those flags are delivered to the client (not dropped from the response).
Each flagged element is marked in the UI on the specific element it concerns, so the user sees which element is flagged.
For each flag, the user can accept it, replace the flagged value with a catalogued one, or request that the value be added to the catalogue.
A flagged element presents an actionable signal to the admin, not a generic error.
The full project gate stays green.
